notes: Reviewed all 62 events in order (sec filings + 11 transcripts) plus 20 fiscal.ai KPIs; quotes verified verbatim against source content.

Skipped 4 fiscal.ai KPIs whose only faithful name would contain the company's own legal/brand name 'Texas Roadhouse' (banned token): 'Company - Texas Roadhouse Restaurant Count', 'Texas Roadhouse - Avg Unit Volume', 'Texas Roadhouse - Comparable Restaurant Sales', 'Texas Roadhouse - Total Store Weeks'; their company-level counterparts (restaurant_count, average_unit_volume, same_store_sales, store_weeks) are in the menu.

Folded 4 franchise count KPIs (Franchise - Texas Roadhouse/Jaggers - US/International Restaurant Count) into one franchise_restaurant_count driver (dedup; TXRH-branded labels also carry the banned company name).

Skipped (vague or unnameable under rules): 53rd-week/additional-operating-week effect (needs number/period token; flows through store_weeks), CFO retirement/succession (person+state naming), generic 'competitive environment', mobile app active users (management could not quantify), supply chain/permitting (bundled, minor), interest-rate boilerplate risk item, utilities and R&M (folded under other_operating_expense), generic consumer-demand commentary (covered by guest_traffic), headline total revenue (outcome; decomposed into traffic/check/store_weeks), ~20 administrative 8-Ks with items-only content (Item 5.02 officer/comp, 5.07 votes, 7.01 Reg FD, 1.01/2.03 credit agreement) carrying no usable prose.
same_store_sales chosen over 'comparable_restaurant_sales' per standard-phrase rule (R6); brand-level variant kept separate as bubbas_33_same_store_sales per R9.
Guidance drivers ({metric}_guidance) coined from recurring 8-K Outlook sections; actual-cost counterparts (commodity_inflation, wage_inflation, capital_expenditures) kept as separate drivers.
